title: Automating the  gpg  Passphrase for signing images in pipelines
body:
Hi team,

I am using this `gpg2 --pinentry-mode=loopback  --passphrase ***  --import private.key`  to automatically load the privatekey into keyring without user intervention for password. 

I am trying to sign the images using the Skopeo 
```
 skopeo copy docker://"${image}" docker://"${image}" --dest-creds iamapikey:"${CLOUD_API_KEY}" --src-creds iamapikey:"${CLOUD_API_KEY}" --sign-by "signer@company.com"
```

This is able to sign my image correctly. But it is asking password in the prompt. Is there any way I can automate this password entering in Skopeo?

comments.body: @akshaykumar-vijapur which password is it prompting you for? For the registry server that you're copying to?  If so, can you do a `skopeo login` to there first?  Or is it a credentials getting requested from you gpg2?

@TomSweeneyRedHat It is not about registry. I'm taking about  GPG password prompt which will be prompted to enter GPG password which was used to sign with GPG keys. 

That prompt can be overridden by using this command 
```
gpg2 --pinentry-mode=loopback --passphrase *** 
```

But not sure weather this is supported by `skopeo copy`.
Thanks, @akshaykumar-vijapur for the info.  I don't believe there's a way to do that yet but sounds like a nice RFE if not.
@TomSweeneyRedHat  Please let me know if I need to open another issue for this RFE ?
@akshaykumar-vijapur Interested in opening a PR to make this happen?
@akshaykumar-vijapur no need for an additional issue, I'll just note RFE in the title for this one and will label it so.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Any timeline for this feature ?
Hi, we are hitting this limitation too...
Linking to this issue: https://github.com/containers/image/issues/1303

Hi, a colleague (@SionArtingstall) and I have finally found a way round this issue:

Firstly install these two packages:
```
yum install expect spawn -y
```

Then run the following to use passphrase from file:
```
expect -c "
spawn skopeo copy --debug --sign-by none@fake.com docker://path/to/image:123 docker://path/to/image:456 --authfile auth.json
expect \"Passphrase: \"
sleep 3
send \"$(cat /path/to/password.txt)\n\"
interact
"
```

or provide as ENV variable:
```
expect -c "
spawn skopeo copy --debug --sign-by none@fake.com docker://path/to/image:123 docker://path/to/image:456 --authfile auth.json
expect \"Passphrase: \"
sleep 3
send \"$MY_PASSPHRASE\n\"
interact
"
```

Obviously the cleaner way is if skopeo enabled us to send passphrase through like gpg does...
@TomSweeneyRedHat Any timelines for this issue. Need this badly!
@akshaykumar-vijapur no current timeline.  Will see if we can get someone to take a look-see.
PRs would be welcome, of course.
Any progress on this issue please?
Thanks for the nudge @rmallam https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1540 WIP PR that will hopefully address the issue.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Pls see https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1540#discussion_r787837813

> Thanks for the nudge @rmallam #1540 WIP PR that will hopefully address the issue.

Thanks for your awesome work @mtrmac @vrothberg . I tested this using the environment variable and it works.
With https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1540 merged, we can close this issue.
